versions.name: unnamed
versions.spec: Databricks migration pipeline: connect to source workspace, export notebooks/jobs/clusters, transform configs, import to target workspace, validate migration

versions.name: unnamed
versions.spec: Databricks migration: connect to source Databricks workspace via API, export all notebooks as .dbc or .ipynb, export jobs JSON, export cluster configs, transform workspace references/URLs/env vars, connect to target Databricks workspace, import notebooks, create jobs, configure clusters, validate output parity between source and target
versions.mermaid: api_call[API Call] --> transform[Transform] --> json_proc[JSON Process] --> validate[Validate]

versions.name: unnamed
versions.spec: Databricks migration pipeline: Connect to source Databricks workspace via REST API (workspace API, jobs API, clusters API, pipelines API), export notebooks as .dbc or .ipynb format, export all job definitions with schedule/triggers/notebook/task configs, export cluster definitions with instance pool configs, export pipeline DLT definitions, transform workspace paths, mount points, secret scopes, and environment variables between source/target, create target workspace resources, import notebooks, recreate jobs/clusters/pipelines with transformed configs, validate by comparing job run outputs, notebook content checksums, and cluster configurations between source and target workspaces

versions.name: unnamed
versions.spec: Databricks migration pipeline: connect to source Databricks workspace via REST API (workspace API for notebooks, jobs API for job definitions, clusters API for cluster configs, pipelines API for DLT pipelines, secrets API for secret scopes), export all notebooks as .dbc archive or individual .ipynb files, export all job definitions including schedules, triggers, timeout settings, retry policies, task graphs, and notebook paths, export all cluster configurations including instance pool IDs, autoscaling settings, spark conf, init scripts, and docker layers, export all DLT pipeline definitions with library dependencies and target tables, export secret scopes and their key-value mappings, transform workspace paths (dbfs:/, /workspace/, mount points), replace source workspace URLs with target workspace URLs in notebook code, map source secret scopes to target secret scopes or Azure Key Vault references, transform cloud-specific configs (AWS S3 buckets, Azure ADLS, GCP GCS), create target workspace resources ma
versions.mermaid: api_call[API Call] --> s3_io[S3 Transfer] --> gcs_io[GCS Transfer] --> transform[Transform] --> validate[Validate] --> test[Test] --> schedule[Schedule] --> retry[Retry]

versions.name: unnamed
versions.spec: Databricks migration pipeline: Connect to source Databricks workspace via REST API (2.0), export all workspace notebooks as .dbc archive or individual .ipynb, export all job definitions (schedules, triggers, tasks, retry policies), export all cluster/instance pool configs (spark conf, init scripts, docker), export all DLT pipeline definitions, export secret scopes and key-value pairs, transform workspace paths and URLs to target workspace, map source secret scopes to target (Azure Key Vault / AWS Secrets Manager), replace cloud-specific storage paths (s3://, adls://, gs://), create corresponding resources in target workspace, import notebooks to target workspace, recreate jobs with transformed notebook paths and cluster refs, recreate clusters with transformed instance pool IDs, recreate DLT pipelines with transformed library paths, validate by comparing source/target job run outputs, notebook content parity, cluster spark conf equivalence, and generate migration report with any warnings
versions.mermaid: api_call[API Call] --> s3_io[S3 Transfer] --> transform[Transform] --> validate[Validate] --> schedule[Schedule] --> retry[Retry]
